{
  "reasoning": "Let me analyze each action:\n\nAction A: The stickman moves to the RIGHT (from left position to right position).\nAction B: The stickman moves to the LEFT (from right position to left position).\nAction C: The stickman jumps UP (moves upward).\n\nNow, looking at the GOAL STATE:\n- The gray (initial) stickman is on the LEFT side\n- The black (current) stickman is on the RIGHT side\n- There is a red obstacle/block on the ground between them, roughly in the middle-left area\n\nThe stickman starts at the initial position (left side, where the gray figure is). The goal is to have the black stickman on the RIGHT side, with a red block between the initial position and the final position.\n\nSo the stickman needs to move from the left to the right, passing over the red block. Since we must avoid the red block and normal physics apply, the stickman needs to jump over it.\n\nThe red block is on the ground between the two positions. To get from left to right while avoiding the red block:\n- The stickman needs to jump (C) to get over the red block, and move right (A).\n\nWait, let me reconsider. The actions seem to be:\n- A: move right one step\n- B: move left one step  \n- C: jump up (in place)\n\nIf the stickman starts at the left (gray position) and needs to reach the right (black position), that's moving right. But there's a red block in the way on the ground.\n\nTo avoid the red block while moving right, the stickman would need to jump over it. So the sequence would be to jump while moving right.\n\nBut looking at the actions more carefully:\n- Action C shows jumping straight up (no horizontal movement)\n- Action A shows moving right\n\nTo jump over an obstacle, in many puzzle games, you'd need to combine jump + move. But here actions seem discrete.\n\nIf C is a jump (up and then comes back down due to gravity), and A moves right, maybe the sequence is:\n- Jump (C) to be in the air, then move right (A) while in the air to clear the obstacle.\n\nOr perhaps: A moves right to be near the block, then C jumps over it (with A again to land on the other side).\n\nActually, looking at the goal image more carefully:\n- The initial gray stickman is at the far left\n- The red block is in the middle\n- The black stickman (goal position) is at the far right\n\nThe stickman needs to move right past the red block. The red block is on the ground, so a simple right movement would collide with it. The stickman needs to jump over it.\n\nA reasonable sequence: C (jump) + A (move right while in air) to clear the block, then land on the right side.\n\nSo the sequence might be: CA or AC\n\nIf we do C first (jump up), then A (move right while in air), the stickman would arc over the red block and land to the right of it. That seems like the shortest sequence: CA\n\nBut wait - does one A move enough to clear the block and reach the final position? Looking at the distances in the image, the final black position seems to be about 2 \"steps\" to the right. But with the red block in between, we need to jump.\n\nI think the shortest sequence is: CA (jump then move right, clearing the obstacle)\n\nActually, let me reconsider whether the jump (C) in itself provides any horizontal movement. From the image, C shows the stickman moving straight up with no horizontal displacement. So C alone doesn't help cross the gap horizontally.\n\nThe combination needed: jump up (C) to clear the height of the red block, and move right (A) to pass over it horizontally.\n\nShortest sequence: CA or AC\n\nGiven physics - you'd typically jump first then move, or move into a jump. I'll go with CA as the answer - jump up first, then move right while airborne to clear the obstacle.\n\nWait, but actually if C is just \"jump in place\" with no horizontal component, and A is \"move right\" - doing CA would mean: jump up, then while in air move right. That could work to get over the block.\n\nAlternatively, A then C would mean move right (toward the block), then jump... but you'd already be at the block.\n\nI think the answer is CA - jump first to get airborne, then move right to clear the red block and land on the other side.",
  "sequence": "CA"
}
"{\"reasoning\":\"Let me analyze each action:\\n\\nAction A: The stickman moves to the RIGHT (from left position to right position).\\nAction B: The stickman moves to the LEFT (from right position to left position).\\nAction C: The stickman jumps UP (moves upward).\\n\\nNow, looking at the GOAL STATE:\\n- The gray (initial) stickman is on the LEFT side\\n- The black (current) stickman is on the RIGHT side\\n- There is a red obstacle/block on the ground between them, roughly in the middle-left area\\n\\nThe stickman starts at the initial position (left side, where the gray figure is). But there's a red block in the way on the ground.\\n\\nTo avoid the red block while moving right, the stickman would need to jump over it. So the sequence would be to jump while moving right.\\n\\nBut looking at the actions more carefully:\\n- Action C shows jumping straight up (no horizontal movement)\\n- Action A shows moving right\\n\\nTo jump over an obstacle, in many puzzle games, you'd need to combine jump + move. But here actions seem discrete.\\n\\nIf C is a jump (up and then comes back down due to gravity), and A moves right, maybe the sequence is:\\n- Jump (C) to be in the air, then move right (A) while in the air to clear the obstacle.\\n\\nOr perhaps: A moves right to be near the block, then C jumps over it (with A again to land on the other side).\\n\\nActually, looking at the goal image more carefully:\\n- The initial gray stickman is at the far left\\n- The red block is in the middle\\n- The black stickman (goal position) is at the far right\\n\\nThe stickman needs to move right past the red block. The red block is on the ground, so a simple right movement would collide with it. The stickman needs to jump over it.\\n\\nA reasonable sequence: C (jump) + A (move right while in air) to clear the block, then land on the right side.\\n\\nSo the sequence might be: CA or AC\\n\\nIf we do C first (jump up), then A (move right while in air), the stickman would arc over the red block and land to the right of it. That seems like the shortest sequence: CA\\n\\nBut wait - does one A move enough to clear the block and reach the final position? Looking at the distances in the image, the final black position seems to be about 2 \\\"steps\\\" to the right. But with the red block in between, we need to jump.\\n\\nI think the shortest sequence is: CA (jump then move right, clearing the obstacle)\\n\\nActually, let me reconsider whether the jump (C) in itself provides any horizontal movement. From the image, C shows the stickman moving straight up with no horizontal displacement. So C alone doesn't help cross the gap horizontally.\\n\\nThe combination needed: jump up (C) to clear the height of the red block, and move right (A) to pass over it horizontally.\\n\\nShortest sequence: CA or AC\\n\\nGiven physics - you'd typically jump first then move, or move into a jump. I'll go with CA as the answer - jump up first, then move right while airborne to clear the obstacle.\\n\\nWait, but actually if C is just \\\"jump in place\\\" with no horizontal component, and A is \\\"move right\\\" - doing CA would mean: jump up, then while in air move right. That could work to get over the block.\\n\\nAlternatively, A then C would mean move right (toward the block), then jump... but you'd already be at the block.\\n\\nI think the answer is CA - jump first to get airborne, then move right to clear the red block and land on the other side.\",\"sequence\":\"CA\"}"

Examine the input images and reason what each of them means and finally come up with the shortest sequence. Your output must be a JSON object with two fields: 'reasoning' and 'sequence'. The 'sequence' field must be a string containing only the capital letters representing the sequence of actions.

Examine the input images and reason what each of them means and finally come up with the shortest sequence. Your output must be a JSON object with two fields: 'reasoning' and 'sequence'. 
                    The 'sequence' field must be a string containing only the capital letters representing the sequence of actions.
Assistant [decoded JSON]
{
  "reasoning": "Let me analyze the actions and the goal state:\n\nAction A: Moves the stickman to the RIGHT (one step right)\nAction B: Moves the stickman to the LEFT (one step left)\nAction C: Makes the stickman JUMP UP (move upward)\n\nGoal state analysis:\n- The stickman starts on the LEFT side (shown in gray as initial position)\n- There is a red obstacle (rectangle) on the ground between the initial position and the goal position\n- The black stickman (final position) is on the RIGHT side, past the red obstacle\n- The stickman needs to avoid the red obstacle\n\nThe stickman needs to move from the left position to the right position (past the red block). Since the red block is on the ground, the stickman needs to jump over it.\n\nLooking at the goal image:\n- The gray stickman is on the left (initial position)\n- The red block is in the middle, on the ground\n- The black stickman is on the right side\n\nThe stickman needs to:\n1. Move right (Action A) to get close to the red block\n2. Jump over the red block (Action C) - but wait, jumping alone might not clear horizontal distance\n3. Continue moving right (Action A) after jumping\n\nActually, let me reconsider. The physics says normal laws apply. The red block is on the ground. The stickman needs to jump over it while moving forward.\n\nLooking at the positions:\n- The stickman appears to need to move about 2 steps to the right to reach the final position\n- The red block is between the initial and final positions\n\nTo jump over the red block, the stickman would need to:\n- Jump (C) while moving right (A), or\n- Move right (A), then jump over the block\n\nWait, if jumping (C) goes straight up, then we need to combine movement. But these are discrete actions.\n\nLet me think about this differently:\n- The stickman starts at the left\n- Needs to end up to the right of the red block\n- The red block is on the ground between start and end positions\n\nIf A moves right one unit and C jumps up, to clear the obstacle while moving right, we might need: A (move right toward block), C (jump while moving? or jump to avoid?), A (move right past block).\n\nActually, with normal physics, if the stickman jumps (C) it goes up and comes back down. To get past the red block, the stickman needs to either jump over it (moving forward during the jump) or there's another way.\n\nLooking at the distances in the goal image:\n- The stickman needs to move roughly 2 units to the right\n- There's a red block in the way on the ground\n\nThe most logical sequence would be:\n- A: move right (approaching the block)\n- C: jump (going up to clear the block - with forward momentum from action A, or the jump itself carries forward)\n- A: move right (landing past the block)\n\nBut wait, if C is a pure vertical jump, we might need: A, C, A - where jumping clears the block while we're in position.\n\nActually, looking more carefully at the goal state, the stickman appears to be about 2 steps to the right of its starting position. The red block takes up about 1 step worth of space.\n\nThe simplest sequence to get past the red block (which is on the ground) would be:\n- Jump over it: We need to be at the block, jump, and land on the other side\n- Sequence: A (move right to block position), C (jump over), A (move right to final position)\n\nOR maybe just: A, C, A = ACA\n\nBut let me check if the jump itself provides horizontal displacement. Looking at Action C, the stickman jumps straight up (the arrow is vertical). So C alone doesn't move horizontally.\n\nSo to jump over the red block:\n1. A - move right (to be near the block)\n2. C - jump up (over the block - if positioned right, gravity brings down on the other side... but that requires the stickman to already be past the block horizontally)\n\nHmm, actually with a pure vertical jump, the stickman would need to be AT the block position, jump straight up, and land in the same spot. This doesn't help pass the block.\n\nMaybe the sequence is: A (move right past block? No, block is in the way), so we need to jump first then move?\n\nWait - if the block is on the GROUND and the stickman can jump OVER it, perhaps:\n- The stickman moves right (A) until adjacent to block\n- Jumps (C) - this allows moving through the block's space while airborne  \n- Lands on the other side\n\nBut if C is purely vertical, the jump goes up and comes back down in the same horizontal position, which means it doesn't help get past a horizontal obstacle.\n\nLet me reconsider the layout. Perhaps the stickman needs to:\n- Jump (C) first to get over the block's height\n- Then move right (A) while in the air? \n\nOr maybe the actions can be combined in sequence where C jumps and A moves simultaneously?\n\nActually, looking at the goal image again - the stickman is to the RIGHT of the red block. The red block appears to be about 1 unit wide. The final position is about 2 units to the right of start.\n\nThe most likely intended solution:\n- A: move right (one step toward block)\n- C: jump (jump over block - maybe the jump has some forward arc)\n- A: move right (one more step to final position)\n\n= ACA\n\nOR:\n- C: jump (to get airborne)\n- A: move right (while in air, passing over block)\n- A: move right (another step to final position)\n\n= CAA\n\nGiven that the jump (C) goes straight up, and we need to avoid the red block on the ground, if the stickman jumps over the block while at the right position, the sequence might be:\n\nA (move right to be at block), C (jump over), A (land and move to final position) = ACA\n\nThis seems like the shortest reasonable sequence of 3 actions. Let me go with ACA.",
  "sequence": "ACA"
}
"{\"reasoning\":\"Let me analyze the actions and the goal state:\\n\\nAction A: Moves the stickman to the RIGHT (one step right)\\nAction B: Moves the stickman to the LEFT (one step left)\\nAction C: Makes the stickman JUMP UP (move upward)\\n\\nGoal state analysis:\\n- The stickman starts on the LEFT side (shown in gray as initial position)\\n- There is a red obstacle (rectangle) on the ground between the initial position and the goal position\\n- The black stickman (final position) is on the RIGHT side, past the red obstacle\\n- The stickman needs to avoid the red obstacle\\n\\nThe stickman needs to move from the left position to the right position (past the red block). Since the red block is on the ground, the stickman needs to jump over it.\\n\\nLooking at the goal image:\\n- The gray stickman is on the left (initial position)\\n- The red block is in the middle, on the ground\\n- The black stickman is on the right side\\n\\nThe stickman needs to:\\n1. Move right (Action A) to get close to the red block\\n2. Jump over the red block (Action C) - but wait, jumping alone might not clear horizontal distance\\n3. Continue moving right (Action A) after jumping\\n\\nActually, let me reconsider. The physics says normal laws apply. The red block is on the ground. The stickman needs to jump over it while moving forward.\\n\\nLooking at the positions:\\n- The stickman appears to need to move about 2 steps to the right to reach the final position\\n- The red block is between the initial and final positions\\n\\nTo jump over the red block, the stickman would need to:\\n- Jump (C) while moving right (A), or\\n- Move right (A), then jump over the block\\n\\nWait, if jumping (C) goes straight up, then we need to combine movement. But these are discrete actions.\\n\\nLet me think about this differently:\\n- The stickman starts at the left\\n- Needs to end up to the right of the red block\\n- The red block is on the ground between start and end positions\\n\\nIf A moves right one unit and C jumps up, to clear the obstacle while moving right, we might need: A (move right toward block), C (jump while moving? or jump to avoid?), A (move right past block).\\n\\nActually, with normal physics, if the stickman jumps (C) it goes up and comes back down. To get past the red block, the stickman needs to either jump over it (moving forward during the jump) or there's another way.\\n\\nLooking at the distances in the goal image:\\n- The stickman needs to move roughly 2 units to the right\\n- There's a red block in the way on the ground\\n\\nThe most logical sequence would be:\\n- A: move right (approaching the block)\\n- C: jump (going up to clear the block - with forward momentum from action A, or the jump itself carries forward)\\n- A: move right (landing past the block)\\n\\nBut wait, if C is a pure vertical jump, we might need: A, C, A - where jumping clears the block while we're in position.\\n\\nActually, looking more carefully at the goal state, the stickman appears to be about 2 steps to the right of its starting position. The red block takes up about 1 step worth of space.\\n\\nThe simplest sequence to get past the red block (which is on the ground) would be:\\n- Jump over it: We need to be at the block, jump, and land on the other side\\n- Sequence: A (move right to block position), C (jump over), A (move right to final position)\\n\\nOR maybe just: A, C, A = ACA\\n\\nBut let me check if the jump itself provides horizontal displacement. Looking at Action C, the stickman jumps straight up (the arrow is vertical). So C alone doesn't move horizontally.\\n\\nSo to jump over the red block:\\n1. A - move right (to be near the block)\\n2. C - jump up (over the block - if positioned right, gravity brings down on the other side... but that requires the stickman to already be past the block horizontally)\\n\\nHmm, actually with a pure vertical jump, the stickman would need to be AT the block position, jump straight up, and land in the same spot. This doesn't help pass the block.\\n\\nMaybe the sequence is: A (move right past block? No, block is in the way), so we need to jump first then move?\\n\\nWait - if the block is on the GROUND and the stickman can jump OVER it, perhaps:\\n- The stickman moves right (A) until adjacent to block\\n- Jumps (C) - this allows moving through the block's space while airborne  \\n- Lands on the other side\\n\\nBut if C is purely vertical, the jump goes up and comes back down in the same horizontal position, which means it doesn't help get past a horizontal obstacle.\\n\\nLet me reconsider the layout. Perhaps the stickman needs to:\\n- Jump (C) first to get over the block's height\\n- Then move right (A) while in the air? \\n\\nOr maybe the actions can be combined in sequence where C jumps and A moves simultaneously?\\n\\nActually, looking at the goal image again - the stickman is to the RIGHT of the red block. The red block appears to be about 1 unit wide. The final position is about 2 units to the right of start.\\n\\nThe most likely intended solution:\\n- A: move right (one step toward block)\\n- C: jump (jump over block - maybe the jump has some forward arc)\\n- A: move right (one more step to final position)\\n\\n= ACA\\n\\nOR:\\n- C: jump (to get airborne)\\n- A: move right (while in air, passing over block)\\n- A: move right (another step to final position)\\n\\n= CAA\\n\\nGiven that the jump (C) goes straight up, and we need to avoid the red block on the ground, if the stickman jumps over the block while at the right position, the sequence might be:\\n\\nA (move right to be at block), C (jump over), A (land and move to final position) = ACA\\n\\nThis seems like the shortest reasonable sequence of 3 actions. Let me go with ACA.\",\"sequence\":\"ACA\"}"

{
  "reasoning": "Let me analyze the actions and the goal state:\n\nAction A: Moves the stickman to the RIGHT (one step right)\nAction B: Moves the stickman to the LEFT (one step left)\nAction C: Makes the stickman JUMP UP (move upward)\n\nNow let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\n- The stickman (black) starts at some initial position\n- In the goal state, the black stickman is on the RIGHT side of the image\n- There is a red obstacle (rectangle) on the ground between the initial position (where the gray ghost stickman is) and the final position\n- The red block is on the ground, between the two positions\n\nSo the stickman needs to get from the left position to the right position, but there's a red block in the way that must be avoided.\n\nLooking at the goal image:\n- The gray (initial/ghost) stickman is on the LEFT\n- The black (final) stickman is on the RIGHT\n- The red rectangle is on the ground between them\n\nThe stickman needs to move from left to right but must avoid the red block. Since the red block is on the ground and physics apply normally, the stickman would need to JUMP OVER the red block.\n\nSo the sequence would be:\n1. Jump (C) - to get over the red block while moving right\n   \nBut wait, jumping (C) moves straight up. To get to the right position, we also need to move right (A).\n\nLet me think about this more carefully:\n\nThe stickman starts at the left position (where the gray ghost is). It needs to reach the right position (where the black stickman is in the goal). There's a red block on the ground between them.\n\nTo move right while avoiding the red block on the ground:\n- Option 1: Jump (C) while moving right (A) - but these might be separate actions\n- The sequence might be: C then A (jump up, then move right while in air, land on right side)\n\nActually, let me reconsider. Looking at action C - it appears the stickman jumps straight up. Action A moves right. \n\nIf the red block is blocking the path on the ground, the stickman needs to:\n1. Jump over the block: This could be done by jumping (C) and then moving right (A), or jumping while moving right.\n\nThe most likely sequence to get from left to right while avoiding the red block on the ground:\n- C (jump up) + A (move right) = jump over the block and land to the right\n\nSo the sequence would be: CA or AC?\n\nSince jumping takes you straight up first, you'd want to: jump (C) then move right (A) while in the air, landing on the other side of the red block.\n\nThe shortest sequence would be: CA\n\nBut wait - how many steps right do we need? Looking at the goal, the black stickman appears to be about 2 steps to the right of the initial position, and the red block takes up some space. \n\nActually, looking more carefully at the positions:\n- The initial position is at the far left\n- The final position is at the far right  \n- The red block spans roughly the middle section of the ground\n\nTo cover the distance and get over the block, it might take: jump (C) and then move right once or twice (A).\n\nGiven the visual, the distance looks like about 1-2 steps. The simplest interpretation: the stickman needs to jump over the red block. \n\nShortest sequence: CA (jump, then move right to clear the block)\n\nActually, thinking about it more - if you jump straight up (C), you'll land in the same spot. You also need to move right (A). But the red block is in between, so you need to coordinate.\n\nThe most logical interpretation for the shortest sequence would be CA or just A if the jump clears it automatically.\n\nBut the key constraint is \"avoid red\" - the red block is on the ground. The stickman needs to be airborne when over the red block.\n\nShortest sequence: CA (C to jump, A to move right while in air / past the block)\n\nActually, I think the answer is just A (move right once) wouldn't work because you'd land on the red block. \n\nLet me reconsider: The stickman starts at the left. The red block is in the middle. The goal has the stickman on the right.\n\nIf A moves right by one unit, and the block spans one unit, and the goal is two units to the right:\n- AA would put you through the red block (not allowed)\n- CA: jump then move right - this clears the block\n\nI'll go with CA as the shortest sequence.",
  "sequence": "CA"
}
"{\"reasoning\":\"Let me analyze the actions and the goal state:\\n\\nAction A: Moves the stickman to the RIGHT (one step right)\\nAction B: Moves the stickman to the LEFT (one step left)\\nAction C: Makes the stickman JUMP UP (move upward)\\n\\nNow let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\\n- The stickman (black) starts at some initial position\\n- In the goal state, the black stickman is on the RIGHT side of the image\\n- There is a red obstacle (rectangle) on the ground between the initial position (where the gray ghost stickman is) and the final position\\n- The red block is on the ground, between the two positions\\n\\nSo the stickman needs to get from the left position to the right position, but there's a red block in the way that must be avoided.\\n\\nLooking at the goal image:\\n- The gray (initial/ghost) stickman is on the LEFT\\n- The black (final) stickman is on the RIGHT\\n- The red rectangle is on the ground between them\\n\\nThe stickman needs to move from left to right but must avoid the red block. Since the red block is on the ground and physics apply normally, the stickman would need to JUMP OVER the red block.\\n\\nSo the sequence would be:\\n1. Jump (C) - to get over the red block while moving right\\n   \\nBut wait, jumping (C) moves straight up. To get to the right position, we also need to move right (A).\\n\\nLet me think about this more carefully:\\n\\nThe stickman starts at the left position (where the gray ghost is). It needs to reach the right position (where the black stickman is in the goal). There's a red block on the ground between them.\\n\\nTo move right while avoiding the red block on the ground:\\n- Option 1: Jump (C) while moving right (A) - but these might be separate actions\\n- The sequence might be: C then A (jump up, then move right while in air, land on right side)\\n\\nActually, let me reconsider. Looking at action C - it appears the stickman jumps straight up. Action A moves right. \\n\\nIf the red block is blocking the path on the ground, the stickman needs to:\\n1. Jump over the block: This could be done by jumping (C) and then moving right (A), or jumping while moving right.\\n\\nThe most likely sequence to get from left to right while avoiding the red block on the ground:\\n- C (jump up) + A (move right) = jump over the block and land to the right\\n\\nSo the sequence would be: CA or AC?\\n\\nSince jumping takes you straight up first, you'd want to: jump (C) then move right (A) while in the air, landing on the other side of the red block.\\n\\nThe shortest sequence would be: CA\\n\\nBut wait - how many steps right do we need? Looking at the goal, the black stickman appears to be about 2 steps to the right of the initial position, and the red block takes up some space. \\n\\nActually, looking more carefully at the positions:\\n- The initial position is at the far left\\n- The final position is at the far right  \\n- The red block spans roughly the middle section of the ground\\n\\nTo cover the distance and get over the block, it might take: jump (C) and then move right once or twice (A).\\n\\nGiven the visual, the distance looks like about 1-2 steps. The simplest interpretation: the stickman needs to jump over the red block. \\n\\nShortest sequence: CA (jump, then move right to clear the block)\\n\\nActually, thinking about it more - if you jump straight up (C), you'll land in the same spot. You also need to move right (A). But the red block is in between, so you need to coordinate.\\n\\nThe most logical interpretation for the shortest sequence would be CA or just A if the jump clears it automatically.\\n\\nBut the key constraint is \\\"avoid red\\\" - the red block is on the ground. The stickman needs to be airborne when over the red block.\\n\\nShortest sequence: CA (C to jump, A to move right while in air / past the block)\\n\\nActually, I think the answer is just A (move right once) wouldn't work because you'd land on the red block. \\n\\nLet me reconsider: The stickman starts at the left. The red block is in the middle. The goal has the stickman on the right.\\n\\nIf A moves right by one unit, and the block spans one unit, and the goal is two units to the right:\\n- AA would put you through the red block (not allowed)\\n- CA: jump then move right - this clears the block\\n\\nI'll go with CA as the shortest sequence.\",\"sequence\":\"CA\"}"

Assistant [decoded JSON]
{
  "reasoning": "Let me analyze each action:\n\nAction A: Moves the stickman to the RIGHT (one step right)\nAction B: Moves the stickman to the LEFT (one step left)\nAction C: Makes the stickman JUMP UP (moves upward)\n\nNow let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\n- The stickman (black) is on the RIGHT side of the image\n- There is a red obstacle (rectangle) on the ground between the left ghost position and the black stickman's current position\n- The ghost (gray) stickman is on the LEFT side\n\nStarting position: The stickman appears to start in the center or some default position. Looking at the actions, the initial position in Action A shows the stickman on the left, and the final is on the right - so A moves right, B moves left.\n\nIn the goal image:\n- The gray ghost is on the LEFT (this represents where the stickman started/came from)\n- The black stickman is on the RIGHT\n- There's a red obstacle on the ground between them\n\nThe stickman needs to get from the left position to the right position, but there's a red obstacle on the ground in between. To avoid the red obstacle, the stickman needs to JUMP over it.\n\nSo the sequence would be:\n1. Jump (C) - to get over the red obstacle\n2. Move right (A) - to land on the right side\n\nWait, let me reconsider. The physics work normally, so:\n- The stickman starts on the left\n- There's a red block on the ground between the start and the goal position\n- To avoid the red block, the stickman needs to jump over it while moving right\n\nIf we just do A (move right), the stickman would land on the red block (which we need to avoid).\n\nSo we need to jump and move right simultaneously, or jump first then move right, or move right then jump...\n\nActually, thinking about it more carefully:\n- C = jump (goes up)\n- A = move right\n\nTo get over the red obstacle while moving right, the sequence could be:\n- C (jump up) then A (move right while in air) - but these might be sequential not simultaneous\n\nOr perhaps:\n- A (move right to be near the obstacle) then C (jump over) then A (land on the right side)\n\nLooking at the goal image more carefully:\n- The ghost is at the far left\n- The black stickman is at the far right\n- The red block is in the middle\n\nThe stickman needs to move right past the obstacle. The simplest path avoiding the red block on the ground would be to jump (C) which moves up, then the stickman would be airborne and move right (A) to clear the obstacle, then land.\n\nActually, with normal physics, jumping (C) goes straight up, then gravity brings it down. To move right AND avoid the red block, you'd need to combine jump with rightward movement.\n\nThe most likely shortest sequence: C then A, or A then C then A.\n\nLooking at the positions - the ghost seems to be one step to the left of where the stickman started (or IS the start), and the black stickman is to the right of the red block.\n\nIf the stickman starts at the ghost position (left), and needs to reach the position to the right of the red block (right side), with a red block on the ground in the middle:\n\nSequence: C (jump) + A (move right) = jump over the obstacle and land on the right\n\nBut these need to be timed. Given the grid-like nature:\n- If the obstacle takes up one grid space, jumping (C) goes up one space, and A moves right one space\n- So CA would jump up then move right (landing position might still be on the obstacle)\n- Or AC would move right (onto obstacle - bad) then jump\n\nPerhaps the answer is just A with a jump: the stickman jumps (C) to get airborne, and then moves right (A) past the obstacle.\n\nThe shortest sequence to move right while avoiding a ground obstacle would be: CA (jump then move right, landing past the obstacle)\n\nActually, I think the intended answer is simply A (move right once) but that would go through the obstacle. \n\nLet me reconsider - maybe the stickman needs to:\n1. Jump (C) over the obstacle\n2. Move right (A) to reach the final position\n\nSo the sequence is CA.\n\nBut wait - jumping straight up (C) and then moving right (A) - if the red block is one unit wide and the stickman jumps straight up, then moves right, it would land on top of or past the block depending on the block's width.\n\nI think the most logical shortest sequence here is: CA\n- C: Jump up (to get over the red obstacle)\n- A: Move right (to land on the right side of the obstacle)\n\nThis gives a sequence of CA.",
  "sequence": "CA"
}
"{\"reasoning\":\"Let me analyze each action:\\n\\nAction A: Moves the stickman to the RIGHT (one step right)\\nAction B: Moves the stickman to the LEFT (one step left)\\nAction C: Makes the stickman JUMP UP (moves upward)\\n\\nNow let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\\n- The stickman (black) is on the RIGHT side of the image\\n- There is a red obstacle (rectangle) on the ground between the left ghost position and the black stickman's current position\\n- The ghost (gray) stickman is on the LEFT side\\n\\nStarting position: The stickman appears to start in the center or some default position. Looking at the actions, the initial position in Action A shows the stickman on the left, and the final is on the right - so A moves right, B moves left.\\n\\nIn the goal image:\\n- The gray ghost is on the LEFT (this represents where the stickman started/came from)\\n- The black stickman is on the RIGHT\\n- There's a red obstacle on the ground between them\\n\\nThe stickman needs to get from the left position to the right position, but there's a red obstacle on the ground in between. To avoid the red obstacle, the stickman needs to JUMP over it.\\n\\nSo the sequence would be:\\n1. Jump (C) - to get over the red obstacle\\n2. Move right (A) - to land on the right side\\n\\nWait, let me reconsider. The physics work normally, so:\\n- The stickman starts on the left\\n- There's a red block on the ground between the start and the goal position\\n- To avoid the red block, the stickman needs to jump over it while moving right\\n\\nIf we just do A (move right), the stickman would land on the red block (which we need to avoid).\\n\\nSo we need to jump and move right simultaneously, or jump first then move right, or move right then jump...\\n\\nActually, thinking about it more carefully:\\n- C = jump (goes up)\\n- A = move right\\n\\nTo get over the red obstacle while moving right, the sequence could be:\\n- C (jump up) then A (move right while in air) - but these might be sequential not simultaneous\\n\\nOr perhaps:\\n- A (move right to be near the obstacle) then C (jump over) then A (land on the right side)\\n\\nLooking at the goal image more carefully:\\n- The ghost is at the far left\\n- The black stickman is at the far right\\n- The red block is in the middle\\n\\nThe stickman needs to move right past the obstacle. The simplest path avoiding the red block on the ground would be to jump (C) which moves up, then the stickman would be airborne and move right (A) to clear the obstacle, then land.\\n\\nActually, with normal physics, jumping (C) goes straight up, then gravity brings it down. To move right AND avoid the red block, you'd need to combine jump with rightward movement.\\n\\nThe most likely shortest sequence: C then A, or A then C then A.\\n\\nLooking at the positions - the ghost seems to be one step to the left of where the stickman started (or IS the start), and the black stickman is to the right of the red block.\\n\\nIf the stickman starts at the ghost position (left), and needs to reach the position to the right of the red block (right side), with a red block on the ground in the middle:\\n\\nSequence: C (jump) + A (move right) = jump over the obstacle and land on the right\\n\\nBut these need to be timed. Given the grid-like nature:\\n- If the obstacle takes up one grid space, jumping (C) goes up one space, and A moves right one space\\n- So CA would jump up then move right (landing position might still be on the obstacle)\\n- Or AC would move right (onto obstacle - bad) then jump\\n\\nPerhaps the answer is just A with a jump: the stickman jumps (C) to get airborne, and then moves right (A) past the obstacle.\\n\\nThe shortest sequence to move right while avoiding a ground obstacle would be: CA (jump then move right, landing past the obstacle)\\n\\nActually, I think the intended answer is simply A (move right once) but that would go through the obstacle. \\n\\nLet me reconsider - maybe the stickman needs to:\\n1. Jump (C) over the obstacle\\n2. Move right (A) to reach the final position\\n\\nSo the sequence is CA.\\n\\nBut wait - jumping straight up (C) and then moving right (A) - if the red block is one unit wide and the stickman jumps straight up, then moves right, it would land on top of or past the block depending on the block's width.\\n\\nI think the most logical shortest sequence here is: CA\\n- C: Jump up (to get over the red obstacle)\\n- A: Move right (to land on the right side of the obstacle)\\n\\nThis gives a sequence of CA.\",\"sequence\":\"CA\"}"

  "reasoning": "Let me analyze each action:\n\nAction A: The stickman moves to the RIGHT (from left position to right position).\nAction B: The stickman moves to the LEFT (from right position to left position).\nAction C: The stickman JUMPS UP (moves upward).\n\nNow let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\n- The stickman (black) is on the RIGHT side of the image\n- There is a red obstacle/block on the ground between the left and right positions\n- The gray figure is on the LEFT side (this is the initial/starting position)\n- The black stickman needs to be on the right side, to the right of the red block\n\nSo the stickman starts on the left side. To get to the right side while avoiding the red block on the ground, the stickman needs to jump over the red block.\n\nStarting position: Left side (where the gray figure is)\nGoal: Right side (where the black figure is), with a red block in between on the ground\n\nTo move from left to right while avoiding the red block:\n- Action A moves right\n- Action C jumps up\n\nIf the stickman just does Action A (move right), it would pass through the red block area. To avoid the red block, the stickman needs to jump over it.\n\nSo the sequence would be: Jump (C) while moving right (A), or jump first then move.\n\nActually, let me think more carefully. The red block is on the ground between the two positions. To get from left to right while avoiding the red block:\n\nOption 1: C (jump up) then A (move right while in air) - but these are discrete actions\nOption 2: A (move right) - but this would go through the red block\n\nWait, looking at physics: if we jump (C) and then move right (A), the stickman would be in the air and could clear the red block.\n\nActually, the simplest interpretation: Jump (C) to go over the red block, and move right (A) to end up on the right side.\n\nThe sequence CA would mean: jump up first, then move right. But with normal physics, jumping and then moving right while in the air could work.\n\nOr AC: move right first (which might not clear the block), then jump.\n\nI think the answer is: C (jump) then A (move right) = CA\n\nBut wait - if C makes the stickman jump straight up, and A makes the stickman move right, then:\n- C: jump up (stickman goes up from left position)\n- A: move right while in air (stickman moves right and lands to the right of the red block)\n\nThis seems like the correct sequence: CA\n\nThe shortest sequence to get from the left to the right of the red block while avoiding it would be CA (jump then move right).",
  "sequence": "CA"
}
"{\"reasoning\":\"Let me analyze each action:\\n\\nAction A: The stickman moves to the RIGHT (from left position to right position).\\nAction B: The stickman moves to the LEFT (from right position to left position).\\nAction C: The stickman JUMPS UP (moves upward).\\n\\nNow let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\\n- The stickman (black) is on the RIGHT side of the image\\n- There is a red obstacle/block on the ground between the left and right positions\\n- The gray figure is on the LEFT side (this is the initial/starting position)\\n- The black stickman needs to be on the right side, to the right of the red block\\n\\nSo the stickman starts on the left side. To get to the right side while avoiding the red block on the ground, the stickman needs to jump over the red block.\\n\\nStarting position: Left side (where the gray figure is)\\nGoal: Right side (where the black figure is), with a red block in between on the ground\\n\\nTo move from left to right while avoiding the red block:\\n- Action A moves right\\n- Action C jumps up\\n\\nIf the stickman just does Action A (move right), it would pass through the red block area. To avoid the red block, the stickman needs to jump over it.\\n\\nSo the sequence would be: Jump (C) while moving right (A), or jump first then move.\\n\\nActually, let me think more carefully. The red block is on the ground between the two positions. To get from left to right while avoiding the red block:\\n\\nOption 1: C (jump up) then A (move right while in air) - but these are discrete actions\\nOption 2: A (move right) - but this would go through the red block\\n\\nWait, looking at physics: if we jump (C) and then move right (A), the stickman would be in the air and could clear the red block.\\n\\nActually, the simplest interpretation: Jump (C) to go over the red block, and move right (A) to end up on the right side.\\n\\nThe sequence CA would mean: jump up first, then move right. But with normal physics, jumping and then moving right while in the air could work.\\n\\nOr AC: move right first (which might not clear the block), then jump.\\n\\nI think the answer is: C (jump) then A (move right) = CA\\n\\nBut wait - if C makes the stickman jump straight up, and A makes the stickman move right, then:\\n- C: jump up (stickman goes up from left position)\\n- A: move right while in air (stickman moves right and lands to the right of the red block)\\n\\nThis seems like the correct sequence: CA\\n\\nThe shortest sequence to get from the left to the right of the red block while avoiding it would be CA (jump then move right).\",\"sequence\":\"CA\"}"

  "reasoning": "\nLet me analyze each action:\n\nAction A: The stickman moves to the RIGHT (from left position to right position)\nAction B: The stickman moves to the LEFT (from right position to left position)\nAction C: The stickman JUMPS UP (moves upward)\n\nNow let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\n- The stickman (black) is on the RIGHT side of the image\n- There is a red obstacle (rectangle) on the ground between the left ghost position and the right position where the stickman currently is\n- The stickman needs to be to the RIGHT of the red block\n- The gray ghost figure is on the LEFT side\n\nSo the starting position appears to be on the LEFT side (where the gray ghost is), and the goal is to be on the RIGHT side of the red obstacle.\n\nThe red block is between the starting position and the goal position. Since we need to avoid the red block and physics apply normally, the stickman needs to JUMP OVER the red block to get to the right side.\n\nStarting position: LEFT\nGoal position: RIGHT (past the red block)\n\nTo get from left to right, normally we'd use Action A (move right). But there's a red block in the way that we need to avoid. Since physics apply normally, we need to jump over it.\n\nSo the sequence would be:\n1. Jump (C) to get over the red block while moving right\n\nBut wait - does jumping alone move the stickman right, or do we need to combine jump with a move right?\n\nLooking at the actions:\n- A: moves right\n- B: moves left  \n- C: jumps up (stays in same horizontal position but goes up)\n\nTo jump over the obstacle while moving right, we might need: A (move right to be near the block), then C (jump) to get over it, then A again to land on the other side.\n\nBut wait, let me reconsider. The goal shows the stickman is to the right of the red block. The starting position (gray ghost) is to the left of the red block.\n\nIf the stickman moves right (A), it would hit the red block (can't pass through it with normal physics). So the stickman needs to jump over the block.\n\nThe sequence could be: C (jump) + A (move right during jump) = but these are sequential actions, not simultaneous.\n\nActually, let me think more carefully. If we use Action A to move right, we'd reach the red block. Then Action C to jump over it. Then Action A to move right to land past it.\n\nBut actually, looking at the images more carefully:\n\nThe goal state shows the stickman is at the right portion of the image, roughly 2 steps to the right of the starting position. The red block is between the start and the goal.\n\nIf A moves one step right, and the block is in the way, we need to jump over it. So:\n- C (jump up) while at starting position - but this doesn't move right\n- We need to move right AND jump\n\nPerhaps the sequence is: A (move right toward block), C (jump over block), A (move right to clear block)... but that's 3 actions.\n\nOr maybe just C alone allows jumping over and landing to the right? No, C seems to jump in place.\n\nWait - maybe the approach is: jump first (C), then during the jump state, move right (A) to clear the block. So: CA\n\nActually, with normal physics, if you jump (C) and then move right (A), you'd move right while in the air, potentially clearing the block.\n\nSo sequence: CA - jump up, then move right while in the air to clear the block and land on the right side.\n\nLet me reconsider the goal position. The stickman in the goal is about 1 step to the right of the red block, which appears to be about 2 positions from the start.\n\nShortest sequence: CA (jump, then move right to clear the obstacle)\n",
  "sequence": "CA"
}

  "reasoning": "Let me analyze each action:\n\nAction A: The stickman moves to the RIGHT (from left position to right position). This is a rightward movement.\n\nAction B: The stickman moves to the LEFT (from right position to left position). This is a leftward movement.\n\nAction C: The stickman JUMPS UP (moves upward). This is a jump action.\n\nNow let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\n- The stickman (black) is on the RIGHT side of the image\n- There is a red obstacle/block on the ground between the left ghost position and the right (current) position\n- The stickman needs to be to the RIGHT of the red block, avoiding it\n\nStarting position: The stickman appears to start in the middle/left area (based on the initial state shown in action A where the gray figure is on the left).\n\nLooking at the goal image:\n- The gray ghost figure is on the LEFT\n- The black stickman is on the RIGHT\n- There's a red block on the ground between them\n\nSo the stickman needs to move from the initial position to the right, past the red block. The red block is on the ground, so simply walking right (Action A) would cause the stickman to hit the red block. The stickman needs to JUMP over the red block.\n\nThe sequence should be: Jump (C) while moving right (A), or jump first then move.\n\nWait, let me reconsider the starting position. The initial position seems to be where the gray ghost is in the goal image - on the LEFT side. The stickman needs to get to the RIGHT side, past the red block.\n\nTo avoid the red block on the ground, the stickman needs to jump over it. So the sequence would be:\n- C (jump) to get over the red block\n- A (move right) to land on the right side\n\nBut actually, since physics apply normally, jumping while moving right would work. Let me think about this more carefully.\n\nIf the stickman starts on the left and needs to get to the right side (past the red block on the ground), the stickman needs to:\n1. Jump (C) - this gets the stickman airborne\n2. While in the air or after landing, move right (A)\n\nOr perhaps:\n1. Move right (A) to get close to the block\n2. Jump (C) to jump over it\n\nBut the shortest sequence would be: jump and move right. Since C jumps upward and A moves right, the combination CA or AC might work.\n\nActually, looking more carefully - the stickman just needs to be on the right side of the red block. The simplest interpretation: starting from left, jump over the red block to land on the right. This would be C (jump) combined with A (move right), so \"CA\" or just jumping over it.\n\nThe shortest sequence to get from left to right while avoiding the red obstacle on the ground would be: C (jump) then A (move right), or A then C... \n\nGiven physics: if you jump (C) and then move right (A), you'd land and then be on the right. But to jump OVER the block, you'd need to be moving right while jumping.\n\nI think the answer is CA - jump first to clear the red block, then move right. Or it could just be A if the block can be avoided by moving right without hitting it... but the block appears to be in the path.\n\nMost likely: the stickman needs to jump over the red block. The sequence is C (jump up) + A (move right) = CA.\n\nWait, but the goal just shows the stickman on the right side of the red block. Starting from the left, to get to the right while avoiding the red block on the ground, the stickman needs to jump over it. So the sequence would be CA (jump then move right) or just A if there's enough clearance, but the block appears to block the path on the ground level.\n\nThe shortest sequence is CA - jump to get over the red block, and move right to land on the other side.",
  "sequence": "CA"
}
"{\"reasoning\":\"Let me analyze each action:\\n\\nAction A: The stickman moves to the RIGHT (from left position to right position). This is a rightward movement.\\n\\nAction B: The stickman moves to the LEFT (from right position to left position). This is a leftward movement.\\n\\nAction C: The stickman JUMPS UP (moves upward). This is a jump action.\\n\\nNow let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\\n- The stickman (black) is on the RIGHT side of the image\\n- There is a red obstacle/block on the ground between the left ghost position and the right (current) position\\n- The stickman needs to be to the RIGHT of the red block, avoiding it\\n\\nStarting position: The stickman appears to start in the middle/left area (based on the initial state shown in action A where the gray figure is on the left).\\n\\nLooking at the goal image:\\n- The gray ghost figure is on the LEFT\\n- The black stickman is on the RIGHT\\n- There's a red block on the ground between them\\n\\nSo the stickman needs to move from the initial position to the right, past the red block. The red block is on the ground, so simply walking right (Action A) would cause the stickman to hit the red block. The stickman needs to JUMP over the red block.\\n\\nThe sequence should be: Jump (C) while moving right (A), or jump first then move.\\n\\nWait, let me reconsider the starting position. The initial position seems to be where the gray ghost is in the goal image - on the LEFT side. The stickman needs to get to the RIGHT side, past the red block.\\n\\nTo avoid the red block on the ground, the stickman needs to jump over it. So the sequence would be:\\n- C (jump) to get over the red block\\n- A (move right) to land on the right side\\n\\nBut actually, since physics apply normally, jumping while moving right would work. Let me think about this more carefully.\\n\\nIf the stickman starts on the left and needs to get to the right side (past the red block on the ground), the stickman needs to:\\n1. Jump (C) - this gets the stickman airborne\\n2. While in the air or after landing, move right (A)\\n\\nOr perhaps:\\n1. Move right (A) to get close to the block\\n2. Jump (C) to jump over it\\n\\nBut the shortest sequence would be: jump and move right. Since C jumps upward and A moves right, the combination CA or AC might work.\\n\\nActually, looking more carefully - the stickman just needs to be on the right side of the red block. The simplest interpretation: starting from left, jump over the red block to land on the right. This would be C (jump) combined with A (move right), so \\\"CA\\\" or just jumping over it.\\n\\nThe shortest sequence to get from left to right while avoiding the red obstacle on the ground would be: C (jump) then A (move right), or A then C... \\n\\nGiven physics: if you jump (C) and then move right (A), you'd land and then be on the right. But to jump OVER the block, you'd need to be moving right while jumping.\\n\\nI think the answer is CA - jump first to clear the red block, then move right. Or it could just be A if the block can be avoided by moving right without hitting it... but the block appears to be in the path.\\n\\nMost likely: the stickman needs to jump over the red block. The sequence is C (jump up) + A (move right) = CA.\\n\\nWait, but the goal just shows the stickman on the right side of the red block. Starting from the left, to get to the right while avoiding the red block on the ground, the stickman needs to jump over it. So the sequence would be CA (jump then move right) or just A if there's enough clearance, but the block appears to block the path on the ground level.\\n\\nThe shortest sequence is CA - jump to get over the red block, and move right to land on the other side.\",\"sequence\":\"CA\"}"

Examine the input images and reason what each of them means and finally come up with the shortest sequence. Your output must be a JSON object with two fields: 'reasoning' and 'sequence'. The 'sequence' field must be a string containing only the capital letters representing the sequence of actions.

Examine the input images and reason what each of them means and finally come up with the shortest sequence. Your output must be a JSON object with two fields: 'reasoning' and 'sequence'. 
                    The 'sequence' field must be a string containing only the capital letters representing the sequence of actions.

{
  "reasoning": "\nLet me analyze each action:\n\nAction A: The stickman moves to the RIGHT (from left position to right position). This is a rightward movement/step.\n\nAction B: The stickman moves to the LEFT (from right position to left position). This is a leftward movement/step.\n\nAction C: The stickman JUMPS UP (the stickman moves upward, as shown by the black figure being higher than the gray initial position).\n\nNow, let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\n- The gray figure (initial position) is on the LEFT side\n- The black figure (current/final position) is on the RIGHT side\n- There is a RED obstacle (a red rectangle) on the ground between them, roughly in the middle-left area\n\nThe stickman needs to move from the initial position (left) to the right side, avoiding the red obstacle on the ground.\n\nStarting position: The stickman starts on the left (where the gray figure is).\n\nThe red block is on the ground between the initial position and the goal position.\n\nTo get from the left to the right while avoiding the red block on the ground, the stickman needs to jump over the red block.\n\nSo the sequence would be:\n- The stickman needs to move right, but there's a red block in the way.\n- Jump (C) to get over the red block, and then move right (A).\n\nWait, let me reconsider. Looking at the goal image:\n- The gray figure is on the far LEFT\n- The black figure (goal position) is on the RIGHT\n- The red block is between them, closer to the middle\n\nIf the stickman starts at the left (gray position), to reach the right (black position), it needs to pass over the red block.\n\nThe approach would be:\n1. Jump (C) - this jumps up but does it also move forward?\n\nLooking at Action C again: The stickman appears to jump upward from the same horizontal position. It doesn't seem to move horizontally.\n\nSo to get past the red block, the stickman would need to:\n- Jump (C) to go over the red block while simultaneously or separately moving right (A)\n\nBut if C is just jumping in place, then the sequence might be:\n- A (move right toward the block) - but this might land on the block\n- C (jump over the block) \n- A (move right to final position)\n\nActually, let me reconsider the positions. The goal shows the black stickman is significantly to the right of the red block, and the gray (start) is to the left of the red block.\n\nLooking at the distances:\n- The red block spans roughly from 1/3 to 2/3 of the image width\n- The black figure is on the right side, past the red block\n\nIf each action A moves one step to the right, and C jumps (in place or forward):\n\nThe stickman starts at the left. The red block is in the middle. The goal is to the right.\n\nPossible sequence: C (jump over the obstacle) then A (move right to reach the goal position)?\n\nOr maybe: A (step right, getting closer to block) + C (jump over block) + A might overshoot...\n\nActually, thinking about it more carefully with physics:\n- The stickman starts at the far left (gray position)\n- Red block is on the ground in the middle area\n- Goal is to have stickman at far right (black position)\n\nThe stickman needs to get from left to right, jumping over the red block. \n\nIf A moves one unit to the right, and C is a jump (upward, possibly covering some horizontal distance too due to physics), then:\n\nLooking at the relative positions - the black figure in the goal seems to be about 2 \"steps\" to the right of the starting position, with the red block in between.\n\nPossible sequences:\n- A + C + A: Move right once, then jump (over the block), then move right once more\n- C + A: Jump (clearing the block from starting position?) then move right\n\nGiven the red block's position appears to be between the 1/3 and 2/3 mark, and the stickman starts at far left...\n\nI think the most logical sequence is: A (step right to get near the block), C (jump over the block), A (step right to final position) = ACA\n\nBut wait - can we do it in 2 steps? If C jumps forward over the block AND the block is right next to the start... \n\nLooking more carefully at the goal image - the gray figure's feet appear to be at roughly x=1/4 of the image, the red block spans from about x=1/3 to x=1/2, and the black figure is at about x=3/4.\n\nSo there seems to be some gap between the initial position and the red block, then the red block, then the goal position.\n\nI'll go with: A (move right, getting adjacent to block), C (jump over block), A (move right to final position) = ACA\n\nThat's 3 actions. Is there a shorter 2-action sequence? \n- CA: Jump then move right - if the jump starts at the left and clears the block, then A moves to final position. This could work if the jump goes over the block from starting position.\n- AC: Move right then jump - might not clear the block properly or might overshoot.\n\nGiven the positions in the image, I think CA might work if the jump arc is large enough to clear the block from the starting position, landing past the block, then one step right to reach the final position.\n\nBut actually, looking at the positions again - the stickman in the goal state is quite far to the right, and the red block is in the middle. The distance from initial to goal seems like it requires 2 rightward movements (A actions). \n\nI'll go with ACA as the most logical sequence - move right to approach, jump over block, move right to final position.\n\nWait, actually let me reconsider. Looking at the goal image more carefully:\n\nThe gray (initial) figure is at far left. The red block is in the middle-left area. The black (goal) figure is at the right.\n\nThe distance from initial to goal looks like it could be 2 steps (A,A) but with a red block in between that must be jumped over.\n\nSo: A (step right), C (jump over block), or just C (jump over block from start) then A (step right to goal).\n\nI think the answer is CA - jump (clearing the red block), then step right to the goal. This is 2 steps which is the shortest possible.\n",
  "sequence": "CA"
}

If C jumps forward over the block AND the block is right next to the start... \\n\\nLooking more carefully at the goal image - the gray figure's feet appear to be at roughly x=1/4 of the image, the red block spans from about x=1/3 to x=1/2, and the black figure is at about x=3/4.\\n\\nSo there seems to be some gap between the initial position and the red block, then the red block, then the goal position.\\n\\nI'll go with: A (move right, getting adjacent to block), C (jump over block), A (move right to final position) = ACA\\n\\nThat's 3 actions. {
  "reasoning": "\nLet me analyze each action:\n\nAction A: The stickman moves to the RIGHT (one step right)\nAction B: The stickman moves to the LEFT (one step left)\nAction C: The stickman JUMPS UP (moves upward, stays in same horizontal position)\n\nNow let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\n- The stickman (black) is on the RIGHT side of the image\n- There is a red obstacle (red rectangle) on the ground between the left ghost position and the right black stickman position\n- The ghost (gray) stickman is on the LEFT side\n- The black stickman needs to be to the RIGHT of the red block, avoiding it\n\nLet me think about the starting position. Looking at the actions:\n- The initial position appears to be center-left area\n- Action A moves right one step\n- Action B moves left one step\n- Action C jumps up\n\nIn the goal image:\n- The ghost (initial position) is on the LEFT\n- The black stickman is on the RIGHT, past the red obstacle\n- The red block is between the ghost position and the black stickman\n\nThe stickman needs to get from the left position to the right position, but there's a red block on the ground in the middle. Since normal physics apply and we must avoid the red block, the stickman needs to JUMP OVER the red block.\n\nSo the sequence would be:\n1. Move right (A) to get close to the block\n2. Jump (C) to go over the block\n3. Move right (A) to land on the other side\n\nWait, let me reconsider. The jump (C) moves the stickman up but what about horizontal movement? Looking at action C, it appears the stickman jumps straight up (no horizontal movement shown). \n\nSo to get past the red block while avoiding it:\n- The stickman needs to jump while moving right, or jump and then move right\n\nBut C only goes up. So the sequence might be:\n- A (move right to be at the block)\n- C (jump up over the block)\n- A (move right to land past the block)\n\nActually, looking more carefully at the goal image - the black stickman appears to be about 2 steps to the right of the initial (ghost) position. The red block is between them.\n\nThe stickman starts at the ghost position. To reach the final position (right of the red block):\n- Move right once (A) - approaching the block\n- Jump (C) - to get over the block  \n- Move right again (A) - to be past the block\n\nThat gives ACA.\n\nBut wait - can we do it in fewer steps? If we jump (C) and then move right (A), that might work if the jump clears the block and then moving right lands past it. But physics-wise, jumping straight up then moving right separately doesn't quite make sense.\n\nActually, let me reconsider. Looking at the goal state more carefully:\n- Ghost is at far left\n- Red block is in the middle-left area\n- Black stickman is at right\n\nThe distance seems to be about 2 steps right from the ghost. The red block is on the ground between positions 1 and 2. \n\nIf the stickman needs to move right twice but there's a block in the way on the ground, they need to jump over it. The shortest path would be: move right (A), jump (C) while at the block, then move right (A) = ACA.\n\nOr perhaps: jump (C) first, then move right (A), then move right (A) = CAA - but jumping in place doesn't help avoid the ground block unless horizontal movement happens during the jump.\n\nGiven normal physics: the stickman would need to jump over the block. The most logical sequence is A (step toward block), C (jump over block), A (step right after landing) = ACA.\n\nActually, I think the simplest interpretation is:\n- A = one step right\n- B = one step left  \n- C = jump (stays in place horizontally but goes up)\n\nTo avoid the red block on the ground while moving right 2 positions:\n- The block appears to be at position 1 (one step right from start)\n- Need to reach position 2 (two steps right from start)\n\nSequence: A (move to position 1... but block is there) - hmm\n\nMaybe: C (jump up at start), A (move right while in air over block), A (move right to final position) = CAA\n\nOr the block spans the middle, so: A (step right, approaching block), C (jump up and over), A (land right of block) = ACA\n\nI'll go with ACA as it seems most physically intuitive - step toward the block, jump over it, step to final position.\n",
  "sequence": "ACA"
}

So the sequence might be:\\n- A (move right to be at the block)\\n- C (jump up over the block)\\n- A (move right to land past the block)\\n\\nActually, looking more carefully at the goal image - the black stickman appears to be about 2 steps to the right of the initial (ghost) position. The red block is between them.\\n\\nThe stickman starts at the ghost position. To reach the final position (right of the red block):\\n- Move right once (A) - approaching the block\\n- Jump (C) - to get over the block  \\n- Move right again (A) - to be past the block\\n\\nThat gives ACA.\\n\\nBut wait - can we do it in fewer steps? If we jump (C) and then move right (A), that might work if the jump clears the block and then moving right lands past it. But physics-wise, jumping straight up then moving right separately doesn't quite make sense.\\n\\nActually, let me reconsider. Looking at the goal state more carefully:\\n- Ghost is at far left\\n- Red block is in the middle-left area\\n- Black stickman is at right\\n\\nThe distance seems to be about 2 steps right from the ghost. The red block is on the ground between positions 1 and 2. \\n\\nIf the stickman needs to move right twice but there's a block in the way on the ground, they need to jump over it. The shortest path would be: move right (A), jump (C) while at the block, then move right (A) = ACA.\\n\\nOr perhaps: jump (C) first, then move right (A), then move right (A) = CAA - but jumping in place doesn't help avoid the ground block unless horizontal movement happens during the jump.\\n\\nGiven normal physics: the stickman would need to jump over the block. The most logical sequence is A (step toward block), C (jump over block), A (step right after landing) = ACA.\\n\\nActually, I think the simplest interpretation is:\\n- A = one step right\\n- B = one step left  \\n- C = jump (stays in place horizontally but goes up)\\n\\nTo avoid the red block on the ground while moving right 2 positions:\\n- The block appears to be at position 1 (one step right from start)\\n- Need to reach position 2 (two steps right from start)\\n\\nSequence: A (move to position 1... but block is there) - hmm\\n\\nMaybe: C (jump up at start), A (move right while in air over block), A (move right to final position) = CAA\\n\\nOr the block spans the middle, so: A (step right, approaching block), C (jump up and over), A (land right of block) = ACA\\n\\nI'll go with ACA as it seems most physically intuitive - step toward the block, jump over it, step to final position.\\n\",\"sequence\":\"ACA\"}"

Examine the input images and reason what each of them means and finally come up with the shortest sequence. Your output must be a JSON object with two fields: 'reasoning' and 'sequence'. The 'sequence' field must be a string containing only the capital letters representing the sequence of actions.

Examine the input images and reason what each of them means and finally come up with the shortest sequence. Your output must be a JSON object with two fields: 'reasoning' and 'sequence'. 
                    The 'sequence' field must be a string containing only the capital letters representing the sequence of actions.
Assistant [decoded JSON]
{
  "reasoning": "\nLet me analyze each action:\n\nAction A: The stickman moves to the RIGHT (from left position to right position). This is a step forward/right.\n\nAction B: The stickman moves to the LEFT (from right position to left position). This is a step backward/left.\n\nAction C: The stickman JUMPS UP (moves upward, staying in same horizontal position). The stickman goes up.\n\nNow let me analyze the GOAL STATE:\n- The gray (initial) stickman is on the LEFT side\n- The black (current) stickman is on the RIGHT side\n- There is a RED obstacle (rectangle) on the ground between them, slightly to the right of center/left area\n\nThe goal shows the stickman has moved to the RIGHT, past the red obstacle. The red obstacle is between the initial position and the final position.\n\nStarting position: The stickman starts at the initial position (left side, where the gray figure is).\n\nTo reach the goal state (right side), the stickman needs to move right past the red obstacle.\n\nSince there's a red obstacle on the ground that must be avoided, the stickman needs to jump over it.\n\nLooking at the positions:\n- Initial position: left side (gray figure)\n- Red obstacle: in the middle-left area\n- Goal position: right side (black figure)\n\nThe stickman needs to:\n1. Move right (Action A) to get close to the obstacle\n2. Jump (Action C) to jump over the obstacle\n3. The stickman would land on the other side\n\nWait, let me reconsider. Looking at the goal image more carefully:\n- The gray stickman is at the far left\n- The red block is in the middle\n- The black stickman is to the right of the red block\n\nSo the stickman needs to move from the left to the right, passing over the red block.\n\nThe sequence would be:\n- Action A: Move right (get closer to obstacle)\n- Action C: Jump over the obstacle\n- Action A: Move right again (land on the other side)\n\nBut actually, let me think about how many steps are needed. Looking at the relative positions:\n\nThe gray stickman is at the far left, the black stickman appears to be about 2 \"steps\" to the right from the initial position, with the red block in between.\n\nIf Action A moves one step right, and the red block is roughly 1-2 steps away:\n- A: move right once (now near the obstacle)\n- C: jump over the obstacle\n- The jump (C) goes up but does it also move forward?\n\nLooking at Action C again: The stickman appears to jump straight up (same horizontal position). So jumping alone won't clear horizontal distance.\n\nActually, with physics: if you jump (C) while moving, you'd need to time it. But in this game context:\n- A = move right one unit\n- B = move left one unit  \n- C = jump (straight up, same position)\n\nTo get past the red obstacle, the stickman might need to: move right to just before the obstacle, jump over it, then the stickman lands on the other side.\n\nBut if C is just a vertical jump with no horizontal movement, we need: A (to be at the obstacle), C (jump over it while at that position)... \n\nActually, thinking about it differently - maybe C causes the stickman to jump and land one position forward (since the stickman is facing right and jumping with forward momentum under normal physics).\n\nLooking at the goal state more carefully:\n- The stickman appears to be 2 positions to the right of the start\n- The red block is between positions 1 and 2 (approximately)\n\nPerhaps the simplest interpretation: A moves right, C jumps (over an obstacle in the path). \n\nSequence: A, C, A or just A, C\n\nIf the stickman starts at position 0, and needs to reach approximately position 2-3 right:\n- The red obstacle seems to be at about position 1-2\n\nLet me think about the minimal sequence:\n- If C is a jump that clears the obstacle and lands on the other side, maybe: A + C + A or A + C\n\nLooking at the image, the black stickman in the goal is roughly 2 step-widths to the right of the gray (start) position, with the red block occupying the space in between.\n\nI'll go with: A (step right once to approach), C (jump over the red block), A (step right to final position) = ACA\n\nBut actually, maybe just jumping while at the start position won't clear... \n\nLet me reconsider: The red block appears to start right after the gray stickman's feet. So:\n- C (jump) at the starting position might jump over part of the block\n- Then A to move to final position\n\nOr: A (step right, now over/at the block) - but that would touch the block.\n\nI think the most logical sequence given normal physics:\n- The stickman needs to jump OVER the red block\n- The red block is just to the right of the starting position\n- So: C (jump up) might not help if we don't also move right\n\nIn many such puzzle games, C might give a jumping arc that moves forward. If C moves diagonally/forward while jumping, then:\n- A: move right (approach obstacle)\n- C: jump over obstacle (clears it and lands past it)\n\nOr simply: C jumps over the obstacle from starting position, then A to reach final position.\n\nGiven the visual, the most logical shortest sequence seems to be: ACA (move right, jump over block, move right to final position)\n\nActually, I think the simplest answer is just \"AC\" - move right once to get close, then jump over the block landing at the goal position. Or \"CA\" - jump from start position to clear the block, then move right.\n\nHmm, let me go with AC as the shortest: A (moves right, approaching the block), C (jumps, clearing the block and landing past it). This would put the stickman approximately where the goal shows.\n\nWait - I need to be more careful. The goal shows the stickman is about 2/3 of the way across the image to the right. The starting position (gray) is about 1/4 from the left. The red block spans from about 1/3 to 1/2 of the image.\n\nShortest sequence: The stickman needs to end up to the right of the red block without touching it.\n\nI'll go with: A (step right once - now at or near the obstacle), C (jump - clears the obstacle), then possibly another A.\n\nBut for shortest: maybe just \"AC\" works - after jumping (C), the stickman lands past the obstacle, then A gets to final position. Or A puts them at the jump point, and C clears the obstacle landing at the goal.\n\nI'll go with \"AC\" as my shortest answer.\n",
  "sequence": "AC"
}

This is a step forward/right.\\n\\nAction B: The stickman moves to the LEFT (from right position to left position). Move right (Action A) to get close to the obstacle\\n2. Jump (Action C) to jump over the obstacle\\n3. The stickman would land on the other side\\n\\nWait, let me reconsider. So jumping alone won't clear horizontal distance.\\n\\nActually, with physics: if you jump (C) while moving, you'd need to time it. But in this game context:\\n- A = move right one unit\\n- B = move left one unit  \\n- C = jump (straight up, same position)\\n\\nTo get past the red obstacle, the stickman might need to: move right to just before the obstacle, jump over it, then the stickman lands on the other side.\\n\\nBut if C is just a vertical jump with no horizontal movement, we need: A (to be at the obstacle), C (jump over it while at that position)... \\n\\nActually, thinking about it differently - maybe C causes the stickman to jump and land one position forward (since the stickman is facing right and jumping with forward momentum under normal physics).\\n\\nLooking at the goal state more carefully:\\n- The stickman appears to be 2 positions to the right of the start\\n- The red block is between positions 1 and 2 (approximately)\\n\\nPerhaps the simplest interpretation: A moves right, C jumps (over an obstacle in the path). \\n\\nSequence: A, C, A or just A, C\\n\\nIf the stickman starts at position 0, and needs to reach approximately position 2-3 right:\\n- The red obstacle seems to be at about position 1-2\\n\\nLet me think about the minimal sequence:\\n- If C is a jump that clears the obstacle and lands on the other side, maybe: A + C + A or A + C\\n\\nLooking at the image, the black stickman in the goal is roughly 2 step-widths to the right of the gray (start) position, with the red block occupying the space in between.\\n\\nI'll go with: A (step right once to approach), C (jump over the red block), A (step right to final position) = ACA\\n\\nBut actually, maybe just jumping while at the start position won't clear... \\n\\nLet me reconsider: The red block appears to start right after the gray stickman's feet. So:\\n- C (jump) at the starting position might jump over part of the block\\n- Then A to move to final position\\n\\nOr: A (step right, now over/at the block) - but that would touch the block.\\n\\nI think the most logical sequence given normal physics:\\n- The stickman needs to jump OVER the red block\\n- The red block is just to the right of the starting position\\n- So: C (jump up) might not help if we don't also move right\\n\\nIn many such puzzle games, C might give a jumping arc that moves forward. If C moves diagonally/forward while jumping, then:\\n- A: move right (approach obstacle)\\n- C: jump over obstacle (clears it and lands past it)\\n\\nOr simply: C jumps over the obstacle from starting position, then A to reach final position.\\n\\nGiven the visual, the most logical shortest sequence seems to be: ACA (move right, jump over block, move right to final position)\\n\\nActually, I think the simplest answer is just \\\"AC\\\" - move right once to get close, then jump over the block landing at the goal position.
